q: Which statement about the hierarchy of subsystems and emergent properties is correct?
options: Emergent properties can be fully predicted by studying each individual component in isolation
Higher levels of biological organization (e.g., organisms) show properties that arise from interactions among subsystems and cannot be seen at lower levels alone
Cells have the same properties as the tissues they form; there are no new properties at higher levels
The nervous system and endocrine system do not interact; they integrate body systems independently
explanation: Emergent properties (e.g., consciousness, immune responses) arise from complex interactions among components; they cannot be predicted or observed at lower levels of organization alone.
